steps.title: Determine angle correspondence
steps.content: Since triangle ABC ∼ triangle DEF with angle A ↔ angle D and angle C ↔ angle F, we have angle B ↔ angle E. Thus angle B = angle E.

steps.title: Use complementary angles to find tan(E)
steps.content:
In right triangle ABC with right angle at C, angles A and B are complementary. For complementary angles, $\tan(B) = \frac{1}{\tan(A)} = \frac{1}{\frac{50}{7}} = \frac{7}{50}$. Since angle E = angle B, we have:
$$\tan(E) = \frac{7}{50} = 0.14$$
